title: MAPLE Act
plain_summary: The MAPLE Act changes a government program that helps low-income seniors buy food. The program gives seniors special coupons to shop at farmers markets and local farm stands. Right now, seniors can use these coupons to buy fresh fruits, vegetables, herbs, and honey. This new law adds maple syrup to the list of foods that seniors can buy with their coupons.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Making Agricultural Products Locally Essential Act or the MAPLE Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Maple Syrup Under the Seniors Farmers' Market Nutrition Program
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of two thousand and two. The change adds maple syrup to the list of foods that seniors can buy with help from the seniors farmers' market nutrition program. The program already lets seniors buy herbs and other foods. Now maple syrup will be added to that list.
